definition: A serverless data-integration service for discovering, preparing, moving, and integrating data for analytics, machine learning, and application development. Includes a Data Catalog, crawlers, ETL jobs (Python or Scala), Glue Studio visual editor, DataBrew, and Schema Registry.
A persistent, fully managed metadata store inside AWS Glue. Used by Athena, EMR, Redshift Spectrum, and Lake Formation as a centralised Hive-compatible catalog of databases, tables, and partitions.

A data-management service that helps catalog, discover, share, and govern data stored across AWS, on-premises, and third-party sources. Provides domain-based data marketplaces with business-friendly search and approval workflows.

An intelligent enterprise search service powered by machine learning. Indexes documents from many connectors (S3, SharePoint, Salesforce, Confluence, etc.) and answers natural-language questions with relevance ranking and read-it-back excerpts.

A time-series-forecasting service that uses ML to deliver accurate forecasts. Bring your historical and related data; Forecast trains models without ML expertise and exposes them via real-time and batch inference APIs.

A real-time personalisation and recommendation service. Trains recipes on your event data, item catalog, and user attributes to deliver tailored recommendations, similar items, personalised rankings, and email/notification segmentation.

Makes it easy to build the human-review workflows often required for machine-learning predictions. Integrates with Textract, Rekognition, and custom SageMaker models; routes low-confidence outputs to human reviewers.
Already documented in Developer Tools batch; merge skips.

Fully managed retrieval-augmented-generation (RAG) workflows for Amazon Bedrock. Connects foundation models to private data sources (S3, web crawl, Confluence, Salesforce) so models answer with current company context, with built-in vector embedding and retrieval.
